key_files.relevance: Framework selection and claude-code default hardcoding
Three locations where claude-code is hardcoded as default
create_wizard_defaults() ensures claude-code always in frameworks
Reference pattern for Ubuntu role (binary download)
Reference pattern for Alpine role (musl binary)
npm install -g pattern for framework installation
Test patterns for framework role inclusion and auto-dependency

patterns.name: Default framework inclusion
patterns.description: Hardcode framework name in wizard.py answers, wizard_integration.py (3 locations), and cli_integration.py create_wizard_defaults()

patterns.name: Framework-to-role mapping
patterns.description: Check config.frameworks list, append role name with underscore (claude-code -> claude_code)

patterns.name: npm-based framework install
patterns.description: Use npm install -g <package> in Ansible role, same command on both distros

patterns.name: Auto-dependency inclusion
patterns.description: If framework needs Node.js and node not in roles, insert after common

patterns.name: Distro variants
patterns.description: Add role to _ROLES_WITH_VARIANTS, create {role}-alpine and {role}-ubuntu dirs

architecture.naming_convention: Config uses kebab-case (codex), role dirs use role-distro (codex-alpine), Python code uses underscore (codex)

testing.commands: make test, make check, make lint, make format

conventions: Framework names: kebab-case in config, underscore in role names
NEVER tailor production code to tests
Use uv for all package management
